item.title: Barriers to mental health care leave many children behind, study cautions
item.description: A new study led by the Harvard Pilgrim Health Care Institute finds that mental health care gaps for U.S. children remain stark: one in five households...

item.title: Sensing local fibers in pancreatic tumors, cancer cells 'choose' to either grow or tolerate treatment
item.description: A feature of pancreatic cancer cells' surroundings determines whether they grow fast or become resistant to chemotherapy, a new study shows. The abili...

item.title: IBD study tracks 54,000 patients, links dysplasia grade to cancer risk
item.description: Precancerous colorectal lesions, or dysplasia, in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) confer markedly different risks of future colorectal ...

item.title: Study finds fewer overdose deaths among needle and syringe program participants
item.description: A recently published study from Karolinska Institutet shows that mortality among people who inject drugs enrolled in the Stockholm needle and syringe ...

item.title: Early periods and changing fertility patterns may influence ovarian cancer risk
item.description: Ovarian cancer is still one of the deadliest gynecological cancers affecting women around the world, especially in East Asian countries, where the num...
